---
title: "LRP86: Does the intervention raise nonword reading via letter-sounds? (off-floor mediation)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP86 is the nonword companion to [LRP59](../lrp-rli-med-059/) (word reading via letter-sounds), testing the code route on the **purest decoding outcome** (#228 item 12). Nonword reading isolates alphabetic decoding with no lexical/whole-word support, so if the intervention's decoding benefit is transmitted by letter-sound knowledge it should surface here. We decompose the intervention's effect on nonword reading into the **natural indirect effect (NIE)** — transmitted by raising letter-sounds $L$ — and the **natural direct effect (NDE)**.

::: {.callout-important title="Off-floor outcome — effects are risk differences"}

Nonword reading is ~57% floored, so — as everywhere in the suite (LRPITT11, LRPDID12, LRPGF11) — the outcome is the **off-floor indicator** ($N_{t2} > 0$), fitted as a Bernoulli. NDE / NIE / total are therefore reported on the **off-floor risk-difference** scale: the change in $\Pr(\text{reads} \ge 1\ \text{nonword})$ attributable to the direct vs the through-letter-sounds path. Off-floor outcomes drop the own-baseline term (the $\mathrm{Normal}(1,\cdot)$ autoregressive prior does not transfer to a binary indicator; a floored baseline logit is degenerate), matching the off-floor ITT/DiD/gain-factor convention.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Also a test of the DAG's own structure"}

The revised DAG (`dag/dag-language-reading.dagitty`) has **no direct $IG \to NW$ edge** — the intervention is drawn to reach nonword reading *only* through the code skills. So a decomposition in which the indirect path carries the effect (NIE $> 0$, NDE $\approx 0$) also corroborates that exclusion restriction; a large direct effect would flag a missing edge.

:::

## Causal structure and phase

Phase 0 only (`phase_mode="itt"`, t1 → t2 — the randomised window; $G = 2 - \text{group}$, positive = intervention helps). Treatment $G$, mediator $M = L_{t2}$ (conditioned on $L_{t1}$), outcome $Y = \mathbf{1}[N_{t2} > 0]$. Joint mediator + off-floor-outcome model with a $G \times M$ interaction; NDE/NIE by counterfactual simulation from the posterior (the g-formula), not a coefficient product. Baseline blending $B_{t1}$ enters as a **covariate confounder** (not the outcome own-baseline) — it reinforces the phonological-awareness-mediated backdoors and adds precision (the LRP66/75 precedent); a graded $L+B$ *two-mediator* decomposition on $N$ remains a separate follow-up.

## Assumptions — read this first

The full mediation-assumption treatment (randomisation handles $G\to Y$/$G\to M$; the binding unverifiable assumption is no unmeasured $L\to N$ confounding; the dose-`IS` exposure-induced-confounder non-identification; the contemporaneous $L_{t2}$/$N_{t2}$ same-wave caveat) is as in [LRP59](../lrp-rli-med-059/#sec-assumptions) — read it there. This is a **model-based g-formula decomposition under stated (cross-world) assumptions, not an identified natural effect**; wide at $n \approx 50$.

**Shared session dose.** Intervention sessions $IS$ (`attend`) are a treatment-induced common cause of the mediator and outcome ($IS \to L$ and $IS \to PA \to N$); following the family precedent they are **not** conditioned on (conditioning on a treatment-affected variable is inadmissible for natural effects), so **shared session dose is a plausible inflator of the NIE via $L$** and the decomposition should be read with that in mind.

::: {.callout-note title="Adjustment set — signed off (2026-07-14), mediation criterion"}

The set blocks common causes of the mediator $L$ and the outcome $N$ **including indirect routes** — arrows *into the mediator* matter, not just parents of the outcome. Adjusted: age $A$; speech production $SP$ (`deapp_c`); phonological memory $RW$ (`erbto`), a common cause via $L \leftarrow RW \to N$; hearing $HS$ (`hs`/`hs_missing`) — **included**, because $L \leftarrow HS \to PA \to N$ is an open route to nonword reading that $RW$ does not block (HS confounds $L \to N$ through blending even though it is not a nonword *parent*); baseline blending $B_{t1}$ (proximal reinforcement + precision); and the mediator baseline $L_{t1}$. **No outcome own-baseline** (off-floor). Provisional pending the time-indexed re-derivation (#250/#264); $E$/$R$ are legitimately absent (no arrows into $L$).

:::

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
